name: Statins for primary prevention
summary: In people without established cardiovascular disease, statins reduce major cardiovascular events, myocardial infarction, stroke, and modestly reduce all-cause mortality. Because baseline risk is lower than in secondary prevention, the relative reductions translate into large numbers-needed-to-treat over a few years.
description: Primary prevention treats people without established cardiovascular disease. Placebo-controlled trials (WOSCOPS, AFCAPS, ASCOT-LLA, JUPITER, MEGA, HOPE-3) and pooled analyses (USPSTF, Cochrane) evaluated statins here. Effects are computed from each trial's event counts; published hazard ratios are very close.
interpretation: These are RCTs, so NNTs are meaningful — but large, because baseline risk is low; read them with their ~2–6 year horizon. Relative reductions are bigger for events (MI, stroke) than for all-cause mortality. The trials shown are a selection of landmark studies and run somewhat stronger than the comprehensive USPSTF meta-analysis (mortality ~17% here vs ~8%); treat USPSTF as the more reliable summary.
methodologyNotes: Risk ratios are computed from each trial's event counts (2×2); published hazard ratios are very close. The USPSTF 2022 pooled estimate is shown per outcome for reference and excluded from pooling. AFCAPS counts are from a secondary summary; MEGA was open-label.

outcomes.label: Major cardiovascular events
outcomes.description: Each trial's primary composite cardiovascular endpoint (definitions vary).

studies.author: JUPITER (Ridker)
studies.citation: Ridker PM, et al. Rosuvastatin to prevent vascular events in men and women with elevated C-reactive protein (JUPITER). N Engl J Med. 2008;359:2195-2207.
studies.population: Elevated hs-CRP, normal LDL; median 1.9 yr
studies.notes: Primary composite (nonfatal MI/stroke, revascularization, unstable-angina hospitalization, CV death). Stopped early. Published HR 0.56 (0.46-0.69).

studies.author: WOSCOPS (Shepherd)
studies.citation: Shepherd J, et al. Prevention of coronary heart disease with pravastatin in men with hypercholesterolemia (WOSCOPS). N Engl J Med. 1995;333:1301-1307.
studies.notes: Primary endpoint: definite nonfatal MI or CHD death.

studies.author: AFCAPS/TexCAPS (Downs)
studies.citation: Downs JR, et al. Primary prevention of acute coronary events with lovastatin (AFCAPS/TexCAPS). JAMA. 1998;279:1615-1622.
studies.notes: First acute major coronary event. Counts from a secondary summary — verify against the original JAMA table.

studies.author: ASCOT-LLA (Sever)
studies.citation: Sever PS, et al. Prevention of coronary and stroke events with atorvastatin in hypertensive patients (ASCOT-LLA). Lancet. 2003;361:1149-1158.
studies.notes: Primary endpoint: nonfatal MI + fatal CHD. Stopped early. Published HR 0.64 (0.50-0.83).

studies.author: HOPE-3 (Yusuf)
studies.citation: Yusuf S, et al. Cholesterol lowering in intermediate-risk persons without cardiovascular disease (HOPE-3). N Engl J Med. 2016;374:2021-2031.
studies.population: Intermediate risk, no CVD; median 5.6 yr
studies.notes: First co-primary composite (CV death, nonfatal MI, nonfatal stroke). Published HR 0.76 (0.64-0.91).

studies.author: MEGA (Nakamura)
studies.citation: Nakamura H, et al. Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease with pravastatin in Japan (MEGA). Lancet. 2006;368:1155-1163.
studies.notes: CHD primary endpoint. Open-label (PROBE) design. Published HR 0.67 (0.49-0.91).

studies.author: USPSTF 2022 (pooled)
studies.citation: Chou R, et al. Statin Use for the Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease in Adults: Updated Evidence Report and Systematic Review for the USPSTF. JAMA. 2022;328(8):754-771.
studies.notes: Pooled composite CV outcome; shown for reference and excluded from pooling to avoid double-counting. USPSTF NNT ≈ 78.

studies.author: JUPITER (Ridker)
studies.citation: Ridker PM, et al. Rosuvastatin to prevent vascular events in men and women with elevated C-reactive protein (JUPITER). N Engl J Med. 2008;359:2195-2207.

studies.author: AFCAPS/TexCAPS (Downs)
studies.citation: Downs JR, et al. Primary prevention of acute coronary events with lovastatin (AFCAPS/TexCAPS). JAMA. 1998;279:1615-1622.
studies.notes: Fatal + nonfatal MI. Counts from a secondary summary — verify.

studies.author: USPSTF 2022 (pooled)
studies.citation: Chou R, et al. Statin Use for the Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease in Adults: Updated Evidence Report and Systematic Review for the USPSTF. JAMA. 2022;328(8):754-771.
studies.notes: Pooled MI; shown for reference, excluded from pooling. USPSTF NNT ≈ 118.

studies.author: JUPITER (Ridker)
studies.citation: Ridker PM, et al. Rosuvastatin to prevent vascular events in men and women with elevated C-reactive protein (JUPITER). N Engl J Med. 2008;359:2195-2207.

studies.author: ASCOT-LLA (Sever)
studies.citation: Sever PS, et al. Prevention of coronary and stroke events with atorvastatin in hypertensive patients (ASCOT-LLA). Lancet. 2003;361:1149-1158.

studies.author: USPSTF 2022 (pooled)
studies.citation: Chou R, et al. Statin Use for the Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease in Adults: Updated Evidence Report and Systematic Review for the USPSTF. JAMA. 2022;328(8):754-771.
studies.notes: Pooled stroke; shown for reference, excluded from pooling. USPSTF NNT ≈ 256.

studies.author: JUPITER (Ridker)
studies.citation: Ridker PM, et al. Rosuvastatin to prevent vascular events in men and women with elevated C-reactive protein (JUPITER). N Engl J Med. 2008;359:2195-2207.
studies.notes: Death from any cause. Published HR 0.80 (0.67-0.97).

studies.author: ASCOT-LLA (Sever)
studies.citation: Sever PS, et al. Prevention of coronary and stroke events with atorvastatin in hypertensive patients (ASCOT-LLA). Lancet. 2003;361:1149-1158.
studies.notes: Death from any cause. Published HR 0.87 (0.71-1.06), not significant.

studies.author: USPSTF 2022 (pooled)
studies.citation: Chou R, et al. Statin Use for the Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease in Adults: Updated Evidence Report and Systematic Review for the USPSTF. JAMA. 2022;328(8):754-771.
studies.notes: Pooled all-cause mortality; shown for reference, excluded from pooling. USPSTF NNT ≈ 286.
